---
title: "Quest 8: Fibonacci - Pattern Practice"
subtitle: "Combine Loops, Variables, and Functions"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🌟 QUEST 8 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/08-fibonacci.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/08-fibonacci.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Fibonacci is great practice for Rust fundamentals.

## 💡 Sequence Rule
Start with `0, 1`. Every next number is the sum of the previous two.

## 👨‍💻 Example

```rust
fn fibonacci(n: usize) -> Vec<u64> {
    let mut seq = vec![0, 1];

    while seq.len() < n {
        let len = seq.len();
        let next = seq[len - 1] + seq[len - 2];
        seq.push(next);
    }

    seq
}

fn main() {
    let result = fibonacci(10);
    println!("{:?}", result);
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
1. Change the program to generate 15 values.
2. Print only the final value.
3. Try printing each value with its index.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn fibonacci(n: usize) -> Vec<u64> {
    let mut seq = vec![0, 1];

    while seq.len() < n {
        let len = seq.len();
        let next = seq[len - 1] + seq[len - 2];
        seq.push(next);
    }

    seq
}

fn main() {
    let result = fibonacci(15);

    for (i, value) in result.iter().enumerate() {
        println!("F({}) = {}", i, value);
    }

    if let Some(last) = result.last() {
        println!("Final value: {}", last);
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 8 Complete
Great work—you combined major Rust basics into one small program.
:::
